description: Migration: comment_threads table created with all columns, PK (tenant, thread_id), CHECK exactly-one of ticket_id/project_task_id, expected FKs
Migration: indexes exist on comment_threads — (tenant, ticket_id, last_activity_at DESC), (tenant, project_task_id, last_activity_at DESC), partial on email_message_id
Migration: comments has nullable thread_id, parent_comment_id, deleted_at columns post-migration with correct FKs
Migration: project_task_comments has nullable thread_id, parent_comment_id, deleted_at columns post-migration with correct FKs
Backfill: every pre-existing comments row has thread_id populated; comment_threads has one row per such comment with root_comment_id=comment_id, reply_count=0
Backfill: comment_threads.email_message_id is set from comment.metadata.email.messageId when present; NULL otherwise
Backfill: running migration twice does not duplicate threads (idempotent)
Backfill: every pre-existing project_task_comments row has thread_id populated; analogous thread row exists
Migration: post-NOT-NULL enforcement, attempting INSERT with NULL thread_id is rejected for both tables
Migration: email_sending_logs has new comment_thread_id column post-migration
Types: ICommentThread compiles and exports all expected fields
Types: IComment has thread_id, parent_comment_id, deleted_at as expected types
Types: IProjectTaskComment has thread_id, parent_comment_id, deleted_at as expected types
Model: createComment with parent_comment_id=NULL creates a new comment_threads row + comment whose thread_id matches; root_comment_id equals new comment_id
Model: createComment with parent_comment_id inherits parent's thread_id; new comment.parent_comment_id matches
Model: createComment rejects when parent_comment_id refers to a comment on a DIFFERENT ticket
Model: createComment rejects when parent_comment_id refers to a soft-deleted parent
Model: createComment rejects internal reply on a client-visible thread root (and vice versa)
Model: inserting a reply increments comment_threads.reply_count by 1 and bumps last_activity_at
Model: deleting a leaf comment hard-deletes the row and decrements reply_count
Model: deleting a root comment that has children soft-deletes (deleted_at set, note replaced with '[deleted]'); children unaffected
Model: findCommentsByTicketId returns thread_id, parent_comment_id, deleted_at for every comment including soft-deleted
Model: createTaskComment with NULL parent creates new comment_threads row with project_task_id set
Model: createTaskComment with parent inherits thread_id and increments counters
Model: deleteTaskComment leaf vs root-with-children behavior matches tickets
Model: getTaskComments returns thread_id, parent_comment_id, deleted_at
Action: commentActions.createComment accepts parent_comment_id in payload and forwards to model
Action: TICKET_COMMENT_ADDED event payload includes thread_id, parent_comment_id, is_reply boolean
Action: client-user attempting to create internal reply is rejected with 403/permission error
Action: projectTaskCommentActions.createTaskComment accepts parent_comment_id and forwards to model
Action: TASK_COMMENT_ADDED event payload includes thread_id, parent_comment_id, is_reply
Action: assertOwnCommentOrInternalUser allows client to delete own reply; rejects deleting another client's reply
Email inbound: reply token in body resolves to a comment_id; resulting comment lands in same thread with parent=latest comment in that thread
Email inbound: In-Reply-To matching email_sending_logs.rfc_message_id resolves to that comment's thread
Email inbound: References[] chain walked end-to-start; first match wins; resolves to expected thread
Email inbound: provider thread id exact match on comment_threads.email_provider_thread_id resolves to that thread
Email inbound: when no resolution strategy matches but ticket matches (subject heuristic / email_metadata), a new top-level thread is created on that ticket
Email inbound: resolution precedence — reply token wins over In-Reply-To when both present
Email outbound: top-level send generates a fresh RFC Message-ID and writes it to comment_threads.email_message_id; outgoing email has no In-Reply-To header
Email outbound: reply in existing thread sets In-Reply-To to the latest outbound rfc_message_id stored in email_sending_logs for that comment_thread_id
Email outbound: References header is comment_threads.email_references + previous Message-ID appended; new entry persisted into the column
Email outbound: a row is created in email_reply_tokens with comment_id of the new outbound comment; recipient_email matches
Email outbound: email_sending_logs row has comment_thread_id, rfc_message_id, reply_token_hash, provider_message_id populated
Email round-trip: outbound + simulated inbound with matching In-Reply-To lands the reply back in the originating thread
UI: CommentThreadList renders one HybridThreadGroup per thread sorted by last_activity_at; respects newestFirst toggle
UI: HybridThreadNode renders child threads recursively; each level past 0 marked as sub-thread
UI: clicking Collapse hides children and switches bar to show Expand + Open in drawer
UI: clicking Open in drawer opens the drawer with the right root + replies
UI: InlineReplyComposer shows Mark-as-Internal switch only; no Mark-as-Resolution; is_internal default matches parent's is_internal
UI: Drawer composer submits create with parent_comment_id; drawer closes; inline list shows new reply after close
UI: depth-4 CSS cap — indent does not grow past depth 4 even with depth-5 data
UI: sub-thread bar (depth >= 1) uses dashed border + white background; depth-0 uses solid grey background
UI: Reply button only visible on hover; keyboard-accessible focus also reveals it (.c-actions opacity rules)
UI: soft-deleted comments render '[deleted]' placeholder with reduced opacity; no Reply button
UI: tab counts at thread level — All counts threads, Client = threads whose root non-internal, Internal = threads whose root internal, Resolution = threads with any is_resolution=true comment
UI: ticket thread sort order toggle re-sorts threads by last_activity_at; within-thread replies still chronological regardless of toggle
Playwright e2e (ticket): hover comment → Reply button appears → click → inline composer opens
Playwright e2e (ticket): submit inline reply → indented child renders under parent with thread bar showing 1 reply
Playwright e2e (ticket): collapse a thread → children hide → bar shows Expand + Open in drawer; click Expand to restore
Playwright e2e (ticket): click Open in drawer → drawer opens with root + replies; close returns inline view unchanged
Playwright e2e (ticket): reply inside drawer adds the comment; closing drawer shows the new reply inline
Playwright e2e (ticket): reply to a reply spawns a sub-thread bar with dashed border
Playwright e2e (ticket): tab switch to Internal hides client-rooted threads; counts update accordingly
Playwright e2e (project task): hover comment → Reply button → inline composer → submit → child indents
Playwright e2e (project task): collapse + Open in drawer behavior identical to ticket flow
Playwright e2e (project task): is_internal toggle absent from task reply composer (tasks have no internal/client split)
Regression: existing ticket with only legacy comments renders identically to today — no thread bars, no indent, layout unchanged
Regression: existing project task with legacy comments renders unchanged
Regression: editing a legacy comment still works; updated_at advanced; tree unaffected
Regression: reactions on comments continue to attach via commentReactionActions; reactions allowed on replies too
Performance: opening a ticket with 100 threads x 5 replies each renders within current SLA budget (existing perf test threshold)
DB integrity: deleting a ticket cascades to comment_threads via FK (and to comments via existing FK)
DB integrity: deleting a project_task cascades to comment_threads via FK
DB integrity: CHECK (exactly-one of ticket_id/project_task_id) prevents inserting a thread with both NULL or both set
Email idempotency: receiving the same provider message twice does not duplicate the comment (existing email_processed_messages dedupe still effective with new thread routing)
Email: outbound to multiple recipients issues distinct reply_tokens per recipient as today
Concurrency: two replies created in parallel on the same thread both succeed; reply_count increments to +2; no lost update
RBAC: client user can reply to a client-visible thread on their own ticket; cannot reply on internal thread; cannot reply on a ticket they don't have access to
Drawer focus management: opening the drawer moves focus into the drawer; closing returns focus to the originating thread bar (matches Radix Dialog focus behavior)
UI: 'Add Comment' top-level submit always creates a new thread (parent_comment_id absent) — never accidentally lands inside the most recent thread
